title: Phagnalon saxatile
intro: Phagnalon saxatile, also known as Saxatile Phagnalon, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and treat wounds. The bioactive constituents of Phagnalon saxatile include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Phagnalon saxatile is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often employed in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Phagnalon saxatile is a species within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Phagnalon, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region. The species is further classified as Phagnalon saxatile (L.) DC., with Carl Linnaeus being credited for its initial description. The full classification of this species is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Phagnalon, species P. saxatile.
